description: Create a content slide on labor productivity versus wage growth using a sidebar-left layout, with four small vertical bar charts by country and a right-side bullet callout panel.

header.headline: Growth rates of labor productivity and real labor income are also decoupling – More innovation does not translate to an equivalent rise in wages
header.sub: Average annual growth rates of labor productivity and average wages, 2010-2019 [%, PP]

items.text: Labor productivity has grown faster than wages across the four countries shown.
The largest decoupling appears in Switzerland, where productivity growth of 1.9% compares with wage growth of 0.9%, implying a 1.0 percentage point gap.
France and Germany also show clear gaps, with productivity at 1.0% and 1.1% versus wage growth at 0.8% and 0.6%, respectively.
The US similarly exhibits a gap between productivity and wage growth, reinforcing that innovation gains do not automatically translate into equivalent wage increases.
